title: Architectural opening covering
abstract: An architectural opening covering includes: a first rail configured to translate laterally in opposite first and second lateral directions; a second rail configured to translate laterally in the opposite first and second lateral directions; a shade material extending between the first and second rails; and a motor drive configured to translate only the first rail selectively in the first and second lateral directions; wherein the second rail is configured to be translated in the first and second lateral directions manually by a user; and wherein the motor drive and the first rail are together configured to translate the second rail in the first lateral direction by the first rail pushing the second rail in the first lateral direction.
claims:
Claims 1. An architectural opening covering including: a first rail having an elongate extent, wherein, with the elongate extent of the first rail extending in a longitudinal direction, the first rail is configured to translate laterally in opposite first and second lateral directions substantially perpendicular to the longitudinal direction; a second rail having an elongate extent, wherein, with the elongate extent of the second rail extending in the longitudinal direction, the second rail is configured to translate laterally in the opposite first and second lateral directions; a shade material extending in the first lateral direction between the first and second rails; and a motor drive configured to translate only the first rail of the first and second rails selectively in the first and second lateral directions; wherein the second rail is configured to be translated selectively in the first and second lateral directions manually by a user; and wherein the motor drive and the first rail are together configured to translate the second rail in the first lateral direction by the first rail pushing the second rail in the first lateral direction. 2. The architectural opening covering of claim 1, further including a third rail configured to be fixed adjacent the architectural opening, wherein the motor drive is provided within the third rail. 3. The architectural opening covering of claim 1 or 2, wherein the motor drive includes a motor and a drive shaft configured to be rotated by the motor about an axis extending in an axial direction, and the architectural opening covering further includes a first elongate flexible member configured to be coupled to the drive shaft and to the first rail, wherein rotation of the drive shaft in a first rotational direction causes the first rail to translate in the first lateral direction, and a second elongate flexible member configured to be coupled to the drive shaft and to the first rail, wherein rotation of the drive shaft in a second rotational direction opposite 47 

to the first rotational direction causes the first rail to translate in the second lateral direction. 4. The architectural opening covering of claim 3, further including first and second spools provided on the drive shaft so as to rotate with the drive shaft, wherein the first elongate flexible member is coupled to the first spool and the second elongate flexible member is coupled to the second spool, and rotation of the drive shaft in the first rotational direction causes the first elongate flexible member to wind around the first spool and the second elongate flexible member to unwind from around the second spool, thereby translating the first rail in the first lateral direction, and rotation of the drive shaft in the second rotational direction causes the second elongate flexible member to wind around the second spool and the first elongate flexible member to unwind from around the first spool, thereby translating the first rail in the second lateral direction. 5. The architectural opening covering of claim 4, further comprising third and fourth spools provided on the drive shaft so as to rotate with the drive shaft, wherein the first elongate flexible member is coupled to the third spool and the second elongate flexible member is coupled to the fourth spool, rotation of the drive shaft in the first rotational direction causes the first elongate flexible member to wind around the third spool and the second elongate flexible member to unwind from around the fourth spool, thereby translating the first rail in the first lateral direction, and rotation of the drive shaft in the second rotational direction causes the second elongate flexible member to wind around the fourth spool and the first elongate flexible member to unwind from around the third spool, thereby translating the first rail in the second lateral direction. 6. The architectural opening covering of claim 5, wherein 48 

 each of the respective first and second elongate flexible members has respective first and second opposite ends, each of the respective first, second, third and fourth spools extends along the drive shaft in the axial direction and has, in the axial direction, respective first and second opposite ends and a respective middle portion connecting the first and second opposite ends, the first end of the first elongate flexible member is coupled to the first end of the first spool, the first elongate flexible member extends between the first and second ends of the first spool, and is held adjacent the second end of the first spool by a first holding member, wherein the first holding member is stationary, the second end of the first elongate flexible member is coupled to the first end of the third spool, the first elongate flexible member extends between the first and second ends of the third spool, and is held adjacent the second end of the third spool by the first holding member, the first end of the second elongate flexible member is coupled to the first end of the second spool, the second elongate flexible member extends between the first and second ends of the second spool, and is held adjacent the second end of the second spool by a second holding member, wherein the second holding member is stationary, the second end of the second elongate flexible member is coupled to the first end of the fourth spool, the second elongate flexible member extends between the first and second ends of the fourth spool, and is held adjacent the second end of the fourth spool by the second holding member, rotation of the drive shaft in the first rotational direction draws the first elongate flexible member through the first holding member so as to wind the first elongate flexible member around the respective middle portions of the first and third spools, and rotation of the drive shaft in the second rotational direction draws the second elongate flexible member through the second holding member so as to wind the second elongate flexible member around the respective middle portions of the second and fourth spools. 7. The architectural opening covering of any one of claims 4 to 6, wherein each spool is substantially cylindrical. 49 8. The architectural opening covering of any one of claims 3 to 7, wherein the first elongate flexible member is coupled to the first rail by a tensioning spring. 9. The architectural opening covering of any of claims 3 to 8, further including a clutch provided between the motor and the drive shaft, wherein the first rail is configured to be translated in the first and second lateral directions manually by a user when the motor and drive shaft are disengaged. 10. The architectural opening covering of any one of claims 3 to 9, further including a guide member configured to guide the first elongate flexible member at an intermediate part of the first elongate flexible member between a part of the first elongate flexible member which is coupled to the drive shaft and a part of the first elongate flexible member which is coupled to the first rail, and to change a direction in which the first elongate flexible member extends, wherein the guide member is configured to be positioned at an end of the architectural opening in the first lateral direction, and the guide member is configured to hold the intermediate part of the first elongate flexible member such that the first elongate flexible member may move through the guide member in a longitudinal direction of the first elongate flexible member. 11. The architectural opening covering of claim 10, wherein the guide member includes a pulley around which the first elongate flexible member is looped. 12. The architectural opening covering of claims 3 to 9, further including a first guide member and a second guide member configured to be positioned at an end of the architectural opening in the first lateral direction, wherein the first guide member is configured to hold a first part of the first elongate flexible member between a part of the elongate flexible member which is coupled to the drive shaft and a part of the first elongate flexible member which is coupled to the first rail, such that the first elongate flexible member may move through the first guide member in a longitudinal direction of the first elongate flexible member, and to change a direction in which the first part of the first elongate flexible member extends, and 50 

 the second guide member is configured to hold a second part of the first elongate flexible member between a part of the first elongate flexible member which is coupled to the drive shaft and a part of the first elongate flexible member which is coupled to the first rail, such that the first elongate flexible member may move through the second guide member in the longitudinal direction of the first elongate flexible member, and to change a direction in which the second part of the first elongate flexible member extends. 13. The architectural opening covering of claim 12, wherein the first guide member includes a pulley around which the first part of the first elongate flexible member is looped and the second guide member includes a pulley around which the second part of the first elongate flexible member is looped. 14. The architectural opening covering of claim 12 or 13, further including a support assembly including a tension cord routing arrangement including tension cords, along which the second rail, and optionally the first rail, is translatable in the first and second lateral directions, and tension cord mounts for receiving the tension cords at the end of the architectural opening in the first lateral direction, wherein the first and second guide members are provided in the tension cord mounts. 15. The architectural opening covering of any one of claims 1 to 13, further including a support assembly configured to hold the second rail, whereinthe support assembly is configured to constrain movement of the second rail other than translation in the first and second lateral directions, and the support assembly is configured to hold the second rail at a position in the first lateral direction such that the second rail is translatable in the first and second lateral directions. 16. The architectural opening covering of claim 15, wherein the support assembly includes a tension cord routing arrangement including tension cords, along which the second rail is translatable in the first and second lateral directions. 17. The architectural opening covering of claim 15, wherein the support assembly is further configured to hold the first rail at a position in the first lateral direction such that 

the first rail is translatable in the first and second lateral directions, and to constrain movement of the first rail other than translation in the first and second lateral directions. 18. The architectural opening covering of claim 17, wherein the support assembly includes a tension cord routing arrangement including tension cords, along which the first and second rails are translatable in the first and second lateral directions. 19. A method of operating an architectural opening covering including using a motor drive to translate, in a first lateral direction, a first rail having an elongate extent in a longitudinal direction substantially perpendicular to the first lateral direction, so as to push a second rail in the first lateral direction, the second rail having an elongate extent in the longitudinal direction. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the method is used to open the architectural opening covering, the method further including prior to pushing the second rail with the first rail, using the motor drive to translate the first rail, in the first lateral direction, towards the second rail, so as to retract a shade material extending between the first and second rails. 21. The method of claim 19, wherein the method is used to close the architectural opening covering, the method further including after pushing the second rail with the first rail, using the motor drive to translate the first rail in the second lateral direction, away from the second rail, so as to extend a shade material extending between the first and second rails. 22. The method of any one of claims 19 to 21, wherein using the motor drive to translate the first rail in the first lateral direction includes rotating a drive shaft by the motor in a first rotational direction, such that a first elongate flexible member winds around a first spool provided on the drive shaft, and a second elongate flexible member simultaneously unwinds from around a second spool provided on the drive shaft, wherein the first elongate flexible member is coupled to the first spool and to the first rail such that winding of the first elongate flexible member around the first spool pulls 

the first rail in the first lateral direction, and the second elongate flexible member is coupled to the second spool and to the first rail such that unwinding of the second elongate flexible member from around the second spool allows the first rail to translate in the first lateral direction. 53
